name: Skill Tree
description: A graph or branching structure where players spend progression resources to unlock abilities or bonuses.
design_notes: Skill trees are planning interfaces and balance structures. The graph shape, prerequisites, refund rules, node visibility, and upgrade costs all shape how players imagine builds. A good tree offers meaningful routes without burying required power in fake choices.
design_purpose: Let players plan character growth across sessions or runs.
Create build identity through mutually reinforcing nodes and prerequisites.
Pace ability unlocks so new verbs and passive bonuses arrive at understandable intervals.
player_fantasy: The player feels they are shaping a build through intentional long-term choices.

parameters.name: node_count
parameters.description: Total unlockable nodes in the tree.

parameters.name: prerequisite_node_ids
parameters.description: Node IDs required before a target node can unlock.

parameters.name: skill_point_cost_curve
parameters.description: Cost progression used as the player moves through the graph.

parameters.name: respec_refund_ratio
parameters.description: Share of spent points returned when the player respecs.

parameters.name: branch_depth_limit
parameters.description: Maximum path depth used to pace powerful unlocks.

edge_cases: Removing a prerequisite node during respec must also remove or disable dependent nodes.
Content patches that move nodes need migration rules for existing saves.
UI navigation must handle large trees with keyboard, mouse, gamepad, and touch.
Mutually exclusive branches require clear warnings before irreversible choices.
common_bugs: A node can be purchased without prerequisites because UI and backend validation differ.
Refunding a node leaves granted abilities or passive stats active.
Circular prerequisites lock the tree or make validation recurse forever.
Localization changes node labels but not search, filters, or controller focus order.
balancing_notes: Avoid mandatory tax nodes that every build must buy unless they serve a clear tutorial purpose.
Powerful synergies need opportunity cost, branch distance, class restriction, or diminishing returns.
accessibility_notes: Support zoom, search, readable focus order, and text scaling for large trees.
Use icons plus text labels so node meaning is not carried only by color or shape.

implementation_notes.unity: Store nodes as ScriptableObjects or JSON, validate graph connectivity in editor tests, and apply unlock effects through data-driven ability grants.
implementation_notes.godot: Represent nodes as Resources with prerequisite arrays and run validation before exporting; keep UI graph layout separate from unlock rules.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use Primary Data Assets or DataTables for nodes and centralize purchase validation in a component or subsystem that also handles save migration.
implementation_notes.web: Load graph data as JSON, compute unlock eligibility in pure functions, and keep visual layout metadata separate from progression state.

example_games.title: Path of Exile
example_games.note: Uses a large passive tree for build planning.

example_games.title: Diablo II
example_games.note: Uses class skill investment to define play style.
